menswear_fit_details_bank_v1.description: Menswear polish intelligence: fit checkpoints, break/taper guidance, collar behavior, shoe hierarchy, and premium finishing logic.

fit_checkpoints_positive: Shoulders look clean—this reads tailored even if it’s casual.
The sleeve length looks on point—very polished.
The torso fit is balanced: not clingy, not floating.
The trouser line looks clean—nice structure.
The overall fit reads intentional, not accidental.
This is a sharp fit without looking try-hard.
The proportions top-to-bottom feel really balanced.
The shirt sits cleanly—no weird pulling.
The pant shape supports the outfit well.
This fit gives that ‘quiet confidence’ menswear vibe.
fit_checkpoints_constructive: This is close—shoulders look fine, but the torso could be a touch cleaner.
Sleeves feel slightly long/short; one small tweak would sharpen it.
The pant line is good, but the taper could be cleaner.
The fit is slightly loose in one area, so the silhouette reads softer than needed.
The shirt is pulling slightly—could be a size/fit tweak.
The trouser break is a bit heavy; it’s making the bottom look less sharp.
The top is clean but the bottom is a little bulky right now.
The proportions are good—just needs one fit refinement to feel premium.
The collar/neck area feels slightly messy; clean collar makes a big difference.
It’s working, but a cleaner fit through one zone would elevate it fast.
trouser_break_guidance: No break (or very minimal break) reads the sharpest and most modern.
A slight break is the safest—clean and wearable.
A full break can look classic, but only if the rest is structured.
If the break is bunching a lot, hemming slightly will make it look premium.
If you want it sleeker, reduce the break and keep the shoe clean.
If you want it relaxed, keep the break but avoid heavy stacking.
Cuffing once can clean up the line without tailoring.
Slim/straight trousers look best with minimal break.
Wider trousers can handle a little break if the drape is clean.
A clean hem instantly makes the outfit look expensive.
taper_and_silhouette: Straight fit reads classic and clean.
Slight taper reads modern without looking tight.
Heavy taper reads very sharp but can feel less relaxed.
If the ankle is too wide, the shoe gets lost—slight taper helps.
If the ankle is too tight, the fit can feel restrictive—straight works better.
For premium vibes: clean line from knee to hem.
If you want more height: keep the line straight and the shoe low-profile.
If you want more structure: pair straight trousers with a structured top layer.
If the silhouette feels boxy, add one structured layer or cleaner taper.
A balanced silhouette is always the win—no extremes needed.
collar_and_shirt_polish: A crisp collar instantly makes it look premium.
If the collar is soft, keeping it neat/open reads intentional.
A slightly open collar reads confident and clean.
If the collar is collapsing, a stiffer fabric or undershirt can help it sit better.
If the shirt is wrinkling a lot, a quick steam is the easiest upgrade.
Clean placket + tidy cuffs = instant polish.
Rolling sleeves once (neatly) reads intentional and relaxed.
If the shirt is long, a partial tuck can clean up proportions fast.
If the shirt is short, leaving it untucked looks cleaner than a messy tuck.
Neck/shoulder cleanliness is the menswear ‘expensive’ signal.
shoe_hierarchy_polish: Dressiest: sleek leather shoe (oxford/derby) → most formal.
Clean casual: minimalist sneaker → modern and sharp.
Relaxed: chunky sneaker → casual, needs a cleaner outfit line to stay polished.
Sandals/slides can work, but the outfit has to be very clean and intentional.
If you want it more premium fast: upgrade the shoe shape first.
A cleaner shoe instantly upgrades even basic outfits.
Match shoe tone with belt/watch strap for a cohesive finish.
If the outfit is loud, keep shoes clean and quiet.
If the outfit is simple, one premium shoe can be the hero.
menswear_finishing_touches: A clean watch is the easiest ‘put together’ signal.
Matching belt + shoe tone makes it feel intentional.
One structured layer (overshirt/jacket) instantly upgrades basics.
Tidy hems and clean sneakers make everything look expensive.
Steam the shirt, clean the shoe—done.
Keep accessories minimal: one hero piece max.
A crisp tee (heavier cotton) reads more premium than a thin one.
A clean haircut/trim finish elevates the outfit without effort.
Texture contrast (one) adds richness: knit + denim, suiting + tee, etc.
If you do one thing: clean shoe + clean collar.

usage_rules.default_pattern: Use 1 menswear insight. Add 1 refinement only if user asks to elevate or says it feels off.
usage_rules.tone_integration: Reaction → Menswear polish observation → Optional refinement → Invitation closer.
